studyMetadata.title: Girma cikin Almasihu (Kashi na 1)
studyMetadata.subtitle: Darasi na almajirantawa
studyMetadata.description: Shin kana girma cikin Almasihu? Allah na son duk ƴaƴansa su yi girma a cikin Yesu. Idan ba ka girma, wannan binciken zai taimaka wajen baiyana menene matsala rashin girman kuma ya za a yi ƙoƙari daidaita al’amuran. Idan kuma ka na girma, wannan binciken zai taimaka wurin ƙara wajen yin girma. Kowane sashe na buƙata amfini da Littafi Mai Tsarki tare da mai karatu ya bincikin kansa ta wurin yin tunani mai zurfi.
studyMetadata.author: Rick Creighton
studyMetadata.shareMessage:
Check out this Bible study app — “Grow in Christ”.

Available soon on Google Play and the App Store.

theme.theme-annotation: Green study theme — muted sage and forest palette. Organic and grounded, evoking growth, new life, and the natural world. Suited to studies on renewal, creation, or spiritual formation. Restful rather than urgent.

elements.passageText5: <sup>15</sup>’The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is near. Repent and believe the gospel! ’

elements.question1: Waɗanne abubuwa biyu ne Yesu ya kira mu mu yi yayin da muke fara rayuwar Kirista?
elements.question3: What two things does Jesus call us to do as we begin the Christian life?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yesu ya kira mu mu yi tuba kuma mu gaskata Bishara. Tuba na nufin juyawa daga zunubi; gaskata na nufin dogaro ga labarin nagari na abin da Yesu ya yi.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus calls us to repent and to believe the gospel. Repentance means turning away from sin; belief means trusting in the good news of what Jesus has done.
elements.questionHint1: Duba umarni biyu da Yesu ya ba a ƙarshen aya — za ka iya faɗa su duka biyun?
elements.questionHint3: Look at the two commands Jesus gives at the end of the verse — can you name them both?

elements.passageText5: <sup>6</sup>Therefore, just as you received Christ Jesus as Lord, continue to live your lives in him, <sup>7</sup>rooted and built up in him and established in the faith just as you were taught, and overflowing with thankfulness.

elements.question1: Ta yaya muke ci gaba da rayuwar Kirista?
elements.question3: How do we continue in the Christian life?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Muna ci gaba da rayuwar Kirista kamar yadda muka fara — ta hanyar karɓar Almasihu a matsayin Ubangiji kuma muna rayuwa a cikinsa. Bulus ya bayyana wannan a matsayin a zama da tushe kuma a ginu a cikin Almasihu, a tabbata cikin imani kamar yadda aka koyar mana, kuma a cika da godiya.
elements.sampleAnswer3: We continue in the Christian life the same way we began it — by receiving Christ as Lord and living in him. Paul describes this as being rooted and built up in Christ, established in the faith as we were taught, and overflowing with thankfulness.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya ce ya kamata mu ci gaba ’daidai kamar yadda’ muka fara — menene wannan ke faɗa muku game da yadda farkon da tsakiyar rayuwar Kirista suke da alaƙa da juna?
elements.questionHint3: Paul says we should continue ’just as’ we began — what does that tell you about how the start and the middle of the Christian life relate to each other?

elements.text1:
Rayuwar Kirista ita ce rayuwar tuba da imani. Haka take farawa, kuma haka ya kamata ta ci gaba. Kiristoci sun fi kyau wajen ci gaba da imani domin ba mu tsammanin imani wani abu ne da ya kamata mu bar shi a baya. Amma wani lokaci mukan ji kunya game da tuba. Muna iya tsammanin cewa tuba abu ne mai kyau a farkon rayuwar Kirista, amma wani abu ne da ya kamata a yi girma daga gare shi.
Kiristoci masu girma na iya jin kunya wajen yarda cewa suna buƙatar yin tuba daga kowane abu. Yayin da ake karɓar zunubi gabaɗaya yana da sauƙi (‘Dukkanmu mun kasance masu zunubi masu buƙatar alheri’) amma ba a taɓe ba (‘Har yanzu ina yin gwagwarmaya da fushi, ko sha’awa…’).
elements.text3:
The Christian life is a life of repentance and faith. That’s how it starts, and that’s how it should continue. Christians are fairly good at continuing with faith because we don’t think faith is something we should leave behind. But we can be embarrassed about repentance. We can think that repentance is great at the start of the Christian life, but that it is something to grow out of.
Mature Christians can be embarrassed to admit that they need to repent of anything. It’s OK to admit sin generally (‘We are all sinners in need of grace’) but not specifically (‘I still struggle with bad temper, or lust…’).

elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>If we say we do not bear the guilt of sin, we are deceiving ourselves and the truth is not in us. <sup>9</sup>But if we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous, forgiving us our sins and cleansing us from all unrighteousness.

elements.question1: Me ya sa matsala ce a yi marmari kamar ba mu da gwagwarmaya da zunubi?
elements.question3: Why is it a problem to pretend we don’t have struggles with sin?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Yohanna ya ce idan muka yi iƙirarin cewa ba mu da zunubi, muna ruɗar da kanmu kuma gaskiya ba ta cikinmu. Yin marmari kamar ba mu da gwagwarmaya da zunubi wani nau’i ne na ruɗar da kai — yana yanke mu daga gafara da tsarkakewa da Allah ke bayarwa ga waɗanda suke furta zunubansu da gaskiya.
elements.sampleAnswer3: John says that if we claim to have no sin, we are deceiving ourselves and the truth is not in us. Pretending we have no struggles with sin is a form of self-deception — it cuts us off from the forgiveness and cleansing that God offers to those who honestly confess.
elements.questionHint1: A cewar aya ta 8, menene ke faruwa a cikinmu idan muka ƙi yarda da zunubanmu — kuma mene ne ba za mu samu ba ta hanyar rashin furta zunubanmu (aya ta 9)?
elements.questionHint3: According to verse 8, what happens inside us when we deny our sinfulness — and what do we miss out on by not confessing (verse 9)?

elements.text1: Yin marmari kamar babu matsaloli, alhalin akwai, yana haifar da munafunci. Yin marmari yana kai ga yin watsi da matsaloli — wanda ke dakatar da girman ruhaniya, kuma yana kafa ka don fāɗuwa. Zunubi yana son duhu. Idan ka ajiye shi a cikin duhu, yana girma. Ya kamata ba haka yake ba. Tuba da imani sune farkon rayuwar Kirista, kuma suna ci gaba da rayuwar Kirista. Da muke yin tuba, muna juya zuwa ga Yesu kuma muna juya daga zunubi — abubuwa biyu suna tafiya tare. Suna tafiya tare a farkon tseren Kirista, haka ma a tsakiya, da kuma har zuwa ƙarshen tseren Kirista. Don haka, yayin da kake yin waɗannan karatuttukan, ka yi muhimmancin imani cikin Yesu, kuma ka yi muhimmancin tuba daga zunubi.
elements.text3: Pretending there are no problems, when of course there are, breeds hypocrisy. Pretending leads to ignoring problems - which stalls spiritual growth, and sets you up for a fall. Sin loves darkness. When you keep it in the dark, it grows. It shouldn’t be like that. Repentance and faith start the Christian life, and they continue the Christian life. When we repent, we turn to Jesus and we turn from sin - the two things go together. They go together at the start of the Christian race, as well as in the middle, and all the way to the end of the Christian race. So, as you do these studies, be serious about faith in Jesus, and be serious about repentance from sin.

elements.passageText5: <sup>5</sup>Now this is the gospel message we have heard from him and announce to you: God is light, and in him there is no darkness at all. <sup>6</sup>If we say we have fellowship with him and yet keep on walking in the darkness, we are lying and not practicing the truth. <sup>7</sup>But if we walk in the light as he himself is in the light, we have fellowship with one another and the blood of Jesus his Son cleanses us from all sin.

elements.question1: Me ya sa yana da muhimmanci a yi gwagwarmaya da zunubi?
elements.question3: Why is it important to fight against sin?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Domin Allah haske ne kuma babu duhu a cikinsa kwata-kwata. Idan muka ci gaba da tafiya cikin zunubi (duhu) yayin da muke iƙirarin yin tarayya da Allah, muna rayuwa cikin ƙarya. Yin gwagwarmaya da zunubi yana da muhimmanci domin shi ne hanyar tarayya ta gaske da Allah da juna, kuma shi ne yadda muke ƙwarewa da tsarkakewa ta jinin Yesu da ke ci gaba.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Because God is light and there is no darkness in him at all. If we keep walking in sin (darkness) while claiming fellowship with God, we are living a lie. Fighting sin matters because it is the path to genuine fellowship with God and with one another, and it is how we experience the ongoing cleansing of Jesus’ blood.
elements.questionHint1: Yohanna ya ce menene ke faruwa ga alaƙarmu da Allah idan muka ci gaba da tafiya cikin duhu — kuma menene ya zama mai yiwuwa idan muka tafiya cikin haske?
elements.questionHint3: What does John say happens to our relationship with God if we keep walking in darkness — and what becomes possible when we walk in the light?

elements.text1: Yaya ’Ginshiƙin Girmarku’ yake?
elements.text3: What does your ’Growth Graph’ look like?

elements.text1:
Idan ba ka girma ba, yana da daraja ka tambayi kanka: ‘Shin gaskiya ne ni Kirista? Shin na juya daga zunubi kuma na juya zuwa ga Almasihu?’. Idan ba ka sanya imaninka cikin Almasihu ba, hakan zai bayyana dalilin da ya sa ba ka girma a cikinsa ba. Amma, idan ka juya daga zunubi zuwa ga Almasihu, duk da haka ba ka girma a cikinsa ba, har yanzu yana da daraja ka tambayi wasu tambayoyi masu wahala. Idan ba ka girma ba, wani abu ba daidai ba ne.
Akwai ginshiƙai guda biyar. Ginshiƙi na farko yana nuna hanyar girma mai kyau sosai. Tsayinku na jiki zai girma haka, amma girman Kirista ba iri ɗaya bane. Akwai nasarori kuma akwai kufai. Akwai hawa kuma akwai sauka. Rayuwar gaske ba ta taɓa zama iri ɗaya haka ba.
elements.text3:
If you are not growing, it’s worth asking: ‘Am I really a Christian? Have I turned from sin and turned to Christ?’. If you haven’t put your faith in Christ, that would explain why you aren’t growing in Him. But, if you have turned from sin to Christ, and yet are not growing in Him, it’s still worth asking some hard questions. If you are not growing, something is wrong.
There are five graphs. The first graph shows a beautifully smooth growth-path. Your physical height would grow like this, but Christian growth isn’t the same. There are successes and there are setbacks. There are ups and there are downs. Real life is never so uniform.

elements.passageText5: <sup>2</sup>For we all stumble in many ways. If someone does not stumble in what he says, he is a perfect individual, able to control the entire body as well.

elements.question1: Shin ya kamata mu sa ran rayuwar Kirista ta zama kyauta daga ’kufai’? (Me ya sa?)
elements.question3: Should we expect the Christian life to be free of ’stumbles’? (Why?)
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: A’a. Yakubu ya faɗi a sarari cewa ’dukkanmu muna kufai ta hanyoyi da yawa.’ Mutum cikakke ne kawai — wanda bai taɓa kufai cikin magana ba — wanda zai sami cikakkiyar iko akan dukkan jikinsa. Tun da babu cikakke, kufai ɓangare ne na al’ada na rayuwar Kirista, ba alama cewa imani ya gaza ba.
elements.sampleAnswer3: No. James says plainly that ’we all stumble in many ways.’ Only a perfect person — someone who never stumbles in speech — would have complete mastery over the whole body. Since no one is perfect, stumbles are a normal part of the Christian life, not a sign that faith has failed.
elements.questionHint1: Yakubu ya yi amfani da kalmar ’dukkanmu’ — menene hakan ke faɗa muku game da wanda ake sa ran ya kufai, da kuma sau nawa?
elements.questionHint3: James uses the word ’all’ — what does that tell you about who is expected to stumble, and how often?

elements.text1: Har ma Ginshiƙi na 2 ba shi da gaskiya ta hanyar da ake iya hasashen ta. Hawansa da saukansa suna da ƙanƙanta kuma suna tsafta. ’Kufayen’ da ke ciki kusan suna da kyau. Rayuwar gaske tana da takaici fiye da haka kuma ba ta iya hasashen ta ba.
elements.text3: Even Graph 2 is unrealistically predictable. Its ups and downs are modest and neat. The ’setbacks’ are almost elegant. Real life is more disappointing and less predictable.

elements.passageText5: <sup>21</sup>So I find this law at work: When I want to do good, evil is present with me. <sup>22</sup>For I delight in the law of God in my inner being. <sup>23</sup>But I see a different law at work in my body—a law that fights against the law of my mind and imprisons me under the law of sin that is at work in my body.

elements.question1: Me ya sa yana da wahala a girma cikin Almasihu?
elements.question3: Why is it hard to grow in Christ?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya bayyana rikici na ciki: har ma yana son yin nagari, mugunta tana cikinsa. Akwai ’shari’ar zunubi’ da ke aiki a jikinsa wanda ke yaƙi da sha’awarsa ta biyayya ga Allah kuma, ta wata hanyar, tana kama shi a kurkuku. Girma cikin Almasihu yana da wahala domin wannan fama ta ciki tana da gaske kuma tana ci gaba — har ma ga muminai masu girma kamar Bulus.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul describes an inner conflict: even when he wants to do good, evil is present within him. There is a ’law of sin’ at work in his body that fights against his desire to obey God and, in a sense, takes him prisoner. Growing in Christ is hard because this internal battle is real and ongoing — even for mature believers like Paul.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya bayyana ƙungiyoyi biyu masu adawa da ke aiki a cikinsa — mene ne su, kuma ta yaya wannan rikici ke bayyana dalilin da ya sa girma fama ce?
elements.questionHint3: Paul describes two opposing forces at work inside him — what are they, and how does that conflict explain why growth is a struggle?

elements.text1: Ginshiƙi na 3 ya fi kusa da abin da zai iya zama ƙwarewar ’al’ada’ ta girman Kirista. Akwai girma, akan lokaci, amma bai yi santsi ba. Akwai ci gaba kuma akwai kufai. A kan ƙaramin ma’aunin lokaci, ba za ka iya koyaushe gaya ko abubuwa suna inganta ko ba haka ba. Amma akan ma’aunin lokaci mai tsawo, za ka iya ganin abin da Ruhu Mai Tsarki ya kasance yana yi a rayuwar wani. Wannan al’ada ce kuma mai kyau.
elements.text3: Graph 3 is closer to what might be a more ’normal’ experience of Christian growth. There is growth, over time, but it is not smooth. There are advances and there are setbacks. On a short timescale, you can’t always tell whether things are improving or not. But over a longer timescale, you can see what the Holy Spirit has been doing in someone’s life. This is normal and good.

elements.alt: Graph 3: A growth chart showing uneven but generally upward spiritual progress over time
elements.caption: Graph 3

elements.text1: Amma wani lokaci mukan ƙare da wani abu mai kama da Ginshiƙi na 4. Ga wannan mutum, akwai girman ruhaniya na ɗan lokaci, amma sai wani abu ya shuɗe. Wataƙila suna iya gano lokacin da ya fara shuɗewa, ko kuma ba su iya ba. Amma, wajen duba baya, a sarari wani abu ya faru. Girmansu ya tsaya.
elements.text3: But sometimes we end up with something more like Graph 4. For this person, there was spiritual growth for a while, but then something went wrong. Maybe they can identify the moment when it started to go wrong, or maybe they can’t. But, looking back, it’s obvious something has happened. Their growth has stalled.

elements.alt: Graph 4: A growth chart showing initial spiritual progress that levels off and stalls
elements.caption: Graph 4

elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>’To the angel of the church in Sardis write: This is the message of the one who holds the seven spirits of God and the seven stars: I know your deeds, that you have a reputation that you are alive, but you are dead. <sup>2</sup>Wake up then, and strengthen what remains that was about to die, for I have not found your deeds complete in the sight of my God. <sup>3</sup>Therefore, remember what you received and heard, and obey it, and repent. If you do not wake up, I will come like a thief, and you will never know at what hour I will come against you.

elements.question1: Shin ya yi daidai a zauna a ’makale’ cikin rayuwar Kirista? (Me ya sa?)
elements.question3: Is it OK to settle for getting ’stuck’ in the Christian life? (Why?)
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: A’a. Kalmomi na Yesu ga ikkilisiyar da ke Sardis suna nuna yadda yake da muhimmanci a zama makale a ruhaniyance. Ya bayyana su a matsayin waɗanda suke da suna na zama da rai amma a gaskiya suna mutuwa — kuma ya yi gargaɗi cewa idan ba su farka ba, su tuba, kuma su koma ga abin da suka fara samu, za a sami sakamakon gaske. Zaɓar ci gaba da tsayuwa ba wata hanya ta tsakiya ce ba.
elements.sampleAnswer3: No. Jesus’ words to the church in Sardis show how serious it is to become spiritually stuck. He describes them as having a reputation for being alive but actually being dead — and warns that if they do not wake up, repent, and return to what they first received, there will be real consequences. Settling for stagnation is not a neutral option.
elements.questionHint1: Yesu ya ce zai yi menene idan wannan ikkilisiya ba ta farka ba — kuma shin hakan ya yi kama da wani abu da ya kamata su ji daɗi da shi?
elements.questionHint3: What does Jesus say he will do if this church does not wake up — and does that sound like something they should be comfortable with?

elements.text1: Wataƙila za ka iya ganin kanka cikin Ginshiƙi na 3. Idan haka ne, waɗannan karatuttukan za su ƙarfafa ka ka ci gaba. Amma wataƙila kai kusa ka ke da Ginshiƙi na 4 (ko wani abu mafi muni)! Addu’ata ita ce, yayin da kake karanta shi, Allah zai yi amfani da waɗannan bayanan don ba ka damar canza hanya. Allah yana so duk ’ya’yansa su girma cikin Yesu. Yana so ka girma cikin Yesu. Idan ka tsaya cikin girmanku, ba dole ya kasance haka ba. Ana iya farawa da girmanku a sake. Kuma, idan ka sake duba idonka a kan Yesu Almasihu, haka zai zama. Ginshiƙi na 4 zai iya zama Ginshiƙi na 5.
elements.text3: Maybe you can identify with Graph 3. If so, these studies will encourage you to keep going. But maybe you are closer to Graph 4 (or to something worse)! My prayer is that, as you read on, God will use these notes to give you a chance to change course. God wants all His children to grow in Jesus. He wants you to grow in Jesus. If you have stalled in your growth, it doesn’t have to stay that way. Your growth can be restarted. And, if you fix your eyes again on Jesus Christ, it will be. Graph 4 can turn into Graph 5.

elements.alt: Graph 5: A growth chart showing stalled spiritual progress that resumes and climbs again
elements.caption: Graph 5

elements.passageText5: <sup>11</sup>And some of you were these things. But you were washed, you were sanctified, you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.

elements.question1: Ta yaya Littafi Mai Tsarki ke ƙarfafa mu, cewa za mu iya girma cikin Almasihu?
elements.question3: How does the Bible encourage us, that we can grow in Christ?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus ya tuna wa Korintiyawa cewa, duk abin da suka kasance, an wanke su, an tsarkake su, kuma an gaskatarwa su a cikin sunan Yesu da kuma ta Ruhu na Allah. Wannan yana da ƙarfafawa sosai: babu zunubi na baya ko salon gazawa da ke bayyana mu. Saboda abin da Almasihu ya riga ya yi, canji na gaske yana yiwuwa — ba mu makale da wanda muka kasance ba.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul reminds the Corinthians that, whatever they used to be, they have been washed, sanctified, and justified in the name of Jesus and by the Spirit of God. This is enormously encouraging: no past sin or pattern of failure defines us. Because of what Christ has already done, genuine change is possible — we are not stuck with who we were.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya ce ’wasu daga cikinku suka kasance waɗannan abubuwa’ — menene lokacin wannan kalmar, kuma menene hakan ke nuna game da yiwuwar canji na gaske?
elements.questionHint3: Paul says ’some of you were these things’ — what tense is that, and what does it imply about the possibility of real change?

elements.passageText5: <sup>6</sup>For I am confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will perfect it until the day of Christ Jesus.

elements.question1: Ta yaya kuma?
elements.question3: How else?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus yana da tabbacin cewa Allah kansa, wanda ya fara aikin nagari a cikinmu, zai ci gaba da shi har zuwa ƙarshensa. Girmanmu bai dogara gaba ɗaya kan ƙoƙarinmu da nufin mu ba — Allah ya ɗauki nauyin kammala abin da ya fara a kowane mai imani, har zuwa ranar Almasihu Yesu.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul is confident that God himself, who started the good work in us, will carry it through to completion. Our growth does not depend solely on our own effort or willpower — God is committed to finishing what he began in every believer, right up to the day of Christ Jesus.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya ce wane ne ya fara aikin nagari a cikin muminai — kuma idan Allah ya fara shi, menene hakan ke faɗa muku game da ko zai kammala shi?
elements.questionHint3: Who does Paul say started the good work in believers — and if God started it, what does that tell you about whether he will see it through?

elements.text1:
Ku saurari labarin Bulus:

Ina makaranta ne lokacin da na fara amincewa da Yesu Almasihu. Na yi farin ciki da yarda da gafara daga Yesu. Rayuwa ta daina kasancewa game da bin dokoki. Bishara ita ce labari mai daɗi sosai da na so in sanar da wasu. Na ƙaunaci bin Yesu. Amma sai al’amura suka fara yin sanyi. Bayan makaranta na so aiki mai riba. Akwai zunúbai da na gwagwarmaya da su. Girman na a matsayin Kirista ya ragu. Ban san me ya yi kuskure ba, ko yadda zan gyara shi ba.
elements.text3:
Listen to Paul’s story:

I was at school when I first put my faith in Jesus Christ. I was glad to be accepted and forgiven by Jesus. Life stopped being about obeying rules. The gospel was such good news that I wanted to make it known to others. I loved following Jesus. But then things started to cool down. After school I wanted a lucrative job. There were sins I struggled with. My growth as a Christian slowed down. I didn’t know what had gone wrong, or how to put it right.

elements.text3: Paul’s story is a common ’Graph 4’ pattern. Jesus does not want that to be your experience, and it doesn’t need to be.

elements.text1: Girma — da Biyayya ga Yesu
elements.text3: Growth — and Obedience to Jesus

elements.text1:
Ka yi tunanin abokin ka ya faɗa cikin tankin najasa. Ya yi kuka, ‘Taimako! Taimako! Cece ni!’ Don haka ka cece shi. Ya ce, ‘Na gode sosai,’ ya shaƙi numfashi, ya kuma yi tsalle ya koma cikin tankin. Sai duk abin ya sake faruwa: ya yi kuka don taimako, ka cece shi, ya shaƙi numfashi, ya kuma yi tsalle ya koma cikin tankin. Sau nawa za ka cece shi?

Sau nawa za ka iya cece shi kafin ka yanke shawara cewa bai da muhimmanci wajen ‘cecewarsa’ daga tankin? Idan yana son ya kasance cikin tankin, ba za a iya cece shi daga tankin ba. Haka lamarin yake tsakaninmu, Yesu, da zunúbi. Yesu yana kiran mu mu zo gare shi domin a cece mu daga zunúbi, laifi, kunya, fushi da hukunci. Amma, sai mun yi sha’awar cecewarmu daga abin da ke kama mu, Yesu baya cece mu. Shi ya sa ceto da almajiranci dole su tafi tare.
elements.text3:
Suppose your friend falls into a septic tank. He calls out, ‘Help! Help! Save me!’ so you pull him out. He says, ‘Thank you very much,‘ recovers his breath, and jumps back in. Then the whole thing repeats: he calls for help, you pull him out, he recovers and then jumps back in again. How many times are you going to pull him out?

How many times can you pull him out before you decide he isn’t serious about being ’saved’ from the tank? If he wants to be in the tank, he can’t be saved from the tank. It’s like that with us, Jesus and sin. Jesus calls us to come to Him to be saved from sin, guilt, shame, wrath, and judgement. But, until we do want to be saved from what holds us captive, Jesus does not save us. That’s why salvation and discipleship must go together.

elements.passageText5: <sup>19</sup>Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit, <sup>20</sup>teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And remember, I am with you always, to the end of the age.’

elements.question1: Babbar Umarni* ba game da ‘tuba’ ba ce. (1) Me take game da shi? (2) Menene daidai abin da almajirai Yesu aka umarce su da su yi biyayya?
elements.question3: The Great Commission* is not about ’conversion’. (1) What is it about? (2) What exactly are Jesus’ disciples commanded to obey?

elements.sampleAnswer1: (1) Babbar Umarni tana game da yin almajirai — mutanen da aka yi wa baftisma kuma aka koyar da su yin biyayya. Tana game da ci gaban koyarwa, ba kawai lokacin yanke shawara ba. (2) Yesu ya ce a koyar da almajiranshi yin biyayya ga duk abin da ya umarce su — cikakkiyar koyarwarsa, ba sashi da aka zaɓa ba.
elements.sampleAnswer3: (1) The Great Commission is about making disciples — people who are baptised and taught to obey. It is about ongoing formation, not just a moment of decision. (2) Jesus says his disciples are to be taught to obey everything he has commanded — the full scope of his teaching, not a selected portion.
elements.questionHint1: Menene babban fi’ili da Yesu ya bayar a aya ta 19 — shin ‘tuba’ ne, ko wani abu ne dabam — kuma koyarwar aya ta 20 ta shafi menene?
elements.questionHint3: What is the main verb Jesus gives in verse 19 — is it ’convert’, or something else — and what does the teaching in verse 20 involve?

elements.question1: Matiyu 28:19-20 galibi ana kiransa da ‘Babbar Umarni’.
elements.question3: Matthew 28:19-20 is often referred to as ’The Great Commission’.

elements.passageText5: <sup>12</sup>My commandment is this: Love one another just as I have loved you. <sup>13</sup>No one has greater love than this—that one lays down his life for his friends. <sup>14</sup>You are my friends if you do what I command you. <sup>15</sup>I no longer call you slaves, because the slave does not understand what his master is doing. But I have called you friends, because I have revealed to you everything I heard from my Father.

elements.question1: Kafin gicciyewarsa, Yesu ya bayyana ma’anar ƙaunarsa da zama abokan sa. Abokantaka da Yesu ta musamman ce. Me ya sa?
elements.question3: Just before his crucifixion, Jesus explains what it means to love him and to be his friends. Friendship with Jesus is unique. Why?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Abokantakar Yesu ta musamman ce saboda dalilai biyu aƙalla. Da farko, abokantaka ce wacce ta kashe shi komai — ya bayyana mafi girman ƙauna a matsayin ba da ransa saboda abokan sa, wanda shi ne abin da ya shirya yi. Na biyu, ba kamar mai mulki wanda ke barin barorinsa cikin duhu ba, Yesu yana raba da abokan sa duk abin da ya ji daga Uba. Abokantaka ce ta babbar sadaka da babbar bayyanawa.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Jesus’ friendship is unique for at least two reasons. First, it is a friendship that cost him everything — he defines the greatest love as laying down one’s life for one’s friends, which is exactly what he is about to do. Second, unlike a master who keeps servants in the dark, Jesus shares with his friends everything he has heard from the Father. It is a friendship of radical sacrifice and radical transparency.
elements.questionHint1: Ta waɗanne hanyoyi biyu Yesu ya bayyana abin da ya sa abokantakarsa ta bambanta da yanayin mai mulki da bawa na yau da kullum?
elements.questionHint3: In what two ways does Jesus describe what makes his friendship different from a typical master-servant relationship?

elements.passageText5: <sup>18</sup>But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the honor both now and on that eternal day.

elements.question1: Girma cikin biyayya ba shi ne kawai fuska ɗaya na girma cikin Yesu ba. Me Bitrus ya ce ya kamata mu girma a ciki? (Ya ambaci abubuwa biyu.)
elements.question3: Growing in obedience is not the only aspect of growing in Jesus. What does Peter say we are to grow in? (He mentions two things.)
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki

elements.sampleAnswer1: Bitrus ya ce ya kamata mu girma cikin alherin da sani na Ubangijinmu Mai Cetonmu Yesu Almasihu. Girma cikin alheri na nufin zurfafa fahimtarmu da kwarewa ta ƙaunar Allah da ba mu ta kyauta. Girma cikin sani na nufin sanin Almasihu sosai — wanda shi ne, abin da ya aikata, da abin da ya kira mu zuwa gare shi.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Peter says we are to grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. Growing in grace means deepening our understanding and experience of God’s undeserved love and acceptance. Growing in knowledge means coming to know Christ more fully — who he is, what he has done, and what he calls us to.
elements.questionHint1: Bitrus ya yi amfani da sunayen suna biyu da aka haɗa da ‘da’ — za ku iya gano su duka, kuma me kuke tsammanin kowanne yana nufin menene?
elements.questionHint3: Peter uses two nouns joined by ’and’ — can you spot both of them, and what do you think each one involves?

elements.text1: Girma cikin alheri ya haɗa da girma cikin fahimtar cewa Allah ya karɓe ku. Ƙaunarsa ba ta dogara da aikin ku ba. Biyayya tana da matsayinta, amma ba ita ce ginshiƙi ba. Allah ba zai daina ƙaunar ku ba. Yana ƙaunar ku ba saboda abin da kuke yi ba, amma saboda abin da Yesu ya riga ya aikata.
elements.text3: Growing in grace includes growing in understanding that you are accepted by God. His love doesn’t depend on your performance. Obedience has a place, but it is not the foundation. God won’t stop loving you. He loves you not because of what you do, but because of what Jesus has already done.

elements.passageText5: <sup>6</sup>For while we were still helpless, at the right time Christ died for the ungodly. <sup>7</sup>(For rarely will anyone die for a righteous person, though for a good person perhaps someone might possibly dare to die.) <sup>8</sup>But God demonstrates his own love for us, in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Mun kasance marasa ƙarfi, masu tsaurin kai ga Allah, kuma masu zunúbi. Ba mu aikata komai don cancanta mutuwar Almasihu ba — a gaskiya, ba mu da ikon taimaka wa kanmu kwata-kwata. Manufar Bulus ita ce ƙaunar Allah ba ta kunna ta hanyar wani abu mai kyau a cikinmu ba; Almasihu ya mutu dominmu daidai lokacin da muke mafi muni.
elements.sampleAnswer3: We were helpless, ungodly, and sinners. We had done nothing to deserve Christ’s death — in fact, we were in no position to help ourselves at all. Paul’s point is that God’s love was not triggered by anything good in us; Christ died for us precisely when we were at our worst.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya yi amfani da kalmomi uku daban-daban don bayyana halinmu lokacin da Almasihu ya mutu — za ku iya gano dukkansu uku, kuma me suke da gama gari?
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses three different words to describe our condition when Christ died — can you find all three, and what do they have in common?

elements.text1: Wannan lokaci yana da muhimmanci. Almasihu bai cece mu ba saboda mun koyi yin masa biyayya! Ya riga ya zo don cece mu, kuma saboda haka muke koyon yin masa biyayya. Muna ƙauna, saboda ya riga ya ƙaunece mu. Muna bauta, saboda ya riga ya bauta.
elements.text3: That timing is important. Christ didn’t save us because we learned to obey Him! He has already come to save us, and because of that we learn to obey Him. We love, because He first loved us. We serve, because He first served.

elements.passageText5: <sup>10</sup>For if while we were enemies we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, how much more, since we have been reconciled, will we be saved by his life?

elements.question1: Me ya sa za mu iya kasancewa da kwarin gwiwa cewa cetonmu ya rigaya ya tabbata?
elements.question3: Why can we be confident that our salvation is already secure?

elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus yana muhawara daga mafi girma zuwa mafi ƙarami: idan Allah ya sulhunta mu da kansa lokacin da muka kasance abokan gābarsa — wanda shi ne mafi wahala — to da yawa fiye da haka tabbataccen ne cewa zai ci gaba da cece mu yanzu da muka zama abokan sa. Cetonmu ya tabbata saboda ya dogara kan abin da Almasihu ya riga ya cim ma, ba kan cancantarmu ta ci gaba ba.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul argues from the greater to the lesser: if God reconciled us to himself when we were his enemies — which was the hardest thing — how much more certain is it that he will keep saving us now that we are his friends? Our salvation is secure because it rests on what Christ has already accomplished, not on our ongoing worthiness.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus yana amfani da hujjar ‘da yawa fiye da haka’ — idan Allah ya aikata mafi wahala lokacin da muka kasance abokan gābarsa, me hakan ke tabbatarwa yanzu da an sulhunta mu?
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses a ’how much more’ argument — if God did the harder thing when we were enemies, what does that logically guarantee now that we are reconciled?

elements.question1: Menene alaƙar da ke tsakanin alheri da tsarki?
elements.question3: What is the connection between grace and godliness?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Alheri ba kawai farkon rayuwar Kirista ba ne — yana koyar da mu. Alherin Allah yana koyar da mu ƙi rashin tsoron Allah da sha’awar duniya, da kuma rayuwa ta hanyar sarrafa kai, adalci, da tsarki. Maimakon ya sa mu yi laifi game da tsarki, alheri shi ne injin da ke samar da tsarki.

elements.questionHint3: The passage says grace ’trains us’ — what exactly does it train us to do, and what does that suggest about the relationship between receiving grace and living a godly life?

elements.text1: Girma — da Sani na Almasihu
elements.text3: Growth — and Knowledge of Christ

elements.text1: Girman ruhaniya ya ƙunshi fiye da ilimi kawai, amma baya ƙunshi ƙasa da haka. Ba za ku iya sanin wani idan ba ku san game da shi ba.
elements.text3: Spiritual growth involves more than just knowledge, but it doesn’t involve less. You cannot know someone if you don’t know about them.

elements.passageText5: <sup>8</sup>More than that, I now regard all things as liabilities compared to the far greater value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things—indeed, I regard them as dung!—that I may gain Christ, <sup>9</sup>and be found in him, not because I have my own righteousness derived from the law, but because I have the righteousness that comes by way of Christ’s faithfulness—a righteousness from God that is in fact based on Christ’s faithfulness. <sup>10</sup>My aim is to know him, to experience the power of his resurrection, to share in his sufferings, and to be like him in his death, <sup>11</sup>and so, somehow, to attain to the resurrection from the dead.

elements.question1: Me ya sa Bulus kansa yana son girma cikin sanin Almasihu?
elements.question3: Why does Paul himself want to grow in knowing Christ?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus yana son sanin Almasihu saboda sanin shi ya zarce darajar duk wani abu da aka haɗa — yana ɗaukar duk tsofaffin nasarorinsa da gataccinsa a matsayin marasa kima idan aka kwatanta. Musamman ma, yana son a samu shi cikin Almasihu tare da adalcin Almasihu (ba na kansa ba), don kwarewa ta ikon tashinsa daga matattu, don raba cikin wahalarsa, da kuma a ƙarshe don cim ma tashin daga matattu. Sanin Almasihu shi ne duk burin.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul wants to know Christ because knowing him surpasses the value of everything else combined — he counts all his former achievements and privileges as worthless by comparison. More specifically, he wants to be found in Christ with Christ’s righteousness (not his own), to experience resurrection power, to share in Christ’s sufferings, and ultimately to attain the resurrection from the dead. Knowing Christ is the whole goal.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus yana jera abubuwa da dama da yana fatan samu ko kwarewansu ta hanyar sanin Almasihu a ayoyi 9-11 — za ku iya ganowa su?
elements.questionHint3: Paul lists several specific things he hopes to gain or experience through knowing Christ in verses 9-11 — can you identify them?

elements.passageText5: this is 13until we all attain to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God—a mature person, attaining to the measure of Christ’s full stature.

elements.question1: Me ya sa Bulus yana son wasu su girma cikin sanin Almasihu?
elements.question3: Why does Paul want others to grow in knowing Christ?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus yana son wasu su girma cikin sanin Ɗan Allah saboda wannan sani yana kai ga balaga — musamman, zuwa cikakkiyar mizanin matsayin Almasihu. Wannan ba burin mutum ɗaya ba ne kawai amma na al’umma: dukan ikkilisiya za su cim ma haɗin kai cikin imani da sani tare, suna girma zuwa cikakkiyar kamalar Almasihu.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul wants others to grow in the knowledge of the Son of God because that knowledge leads to maturity — specifically, to reaching the full measure of Christ’s stature. This is not just a personal goal but a communal one: the whole church is to attain unity in faith and knowledge together, growing up into Christ-like completeness.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya ce menene sakamakon ƙarshe na girma tare cikin sanin Ɗan Allah — kuma shin wannan burin ga daidaikun mutane kawai ne, ko ga dukan ikkilisiya?
elements.questionHint3: What does Paul say is the end result of growing together in knowledge of the Son of God — and is this goal for individuals only, or for the whole church?

elements.text1: Idan muna da muhimmanci wajen sanin Yesu, muna bukatar sanin game da shi. Muna koyon hakan ta hanyar Kalmarsa. Kuma muna bukatar san shi. Muna bukatar bin shi, amincewa da zama Ubangijinsa, da kuma dogara gare shi. Muna girma yayin da muke aiwatar da sanin mu game da Almasihu cikin aiki, da kuma yayin da muke amincewa da shi saboda mun san shi.
elements.text3: If we are serious about knowing Jesus, we need to know about Him. We learn that through His Word. We also need to know Him. We need to follow Him, trust His lordship, and depend on Him. We grow as we put our knowledge about Christ into practice, and as we trust Him because we know Him.

elements.text1: Girma — da Tsufa da Balaga
elements.text3: Growth — and Ageing and Maturity

elements.text1: Girma ba kawai barin lokaci ya wuce ba ne. Tsufa yana zuwa kai tsaye; amma girma ba ya zuwa kai tsaye, kuma balaga ba ta zuwa kai tsaye. Mutum na iya tsufa ba tare da ya girma cikin balaga ko hikima ko tsarki ba. Wasu daga cikinmu suna tsayawa a matsayin Kiristoci jariri. Wannan ba shi ne Yesu yake so a gare mu ba, kuma ba shi ne nufin Allah a gare mu ba.
elements.text3: Growth is about more than just allowing time to pass. Ageing is automatic; growing is not, and maturing is not. You can get older without growing in maturity or wisdom or godliness. Some of us get stuck as baby Christians. That is not what Jesus wants for us, and it is not what God intends for us.

elements.passageText5: <sup>28</sup>We proclaim him by instructing and teaching all people with all wisdom so that we may present every person mature in Christ.

elements.question1: Menene Bulus ke gani a matsayin manufar ƙarshe ta duk koyarwarsa da wa’azinsa?
elements.question3: What does Paul see as the end goal of all his teaching and preaching?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Manufar ƙarshe ta Bulus a duk koyarwarsa da wa’azinsa ita ce ya gabatar da kowane mutum a matsayin mai balaga a cikin Almasihu. Manufarsa ba kawai ta sa mutane su gaskata ba, ko ta ƙara adadin ikilisiya ba — ita ce ta ga kowane mutum ya balaga ƙwarai a cikin dangantakarsa da Almasihu.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul’s ultimate goal in all his teaching and preaching is to present every person mature in Christ. It is not simply to get people to believe, or to grow the church numerically — it is to see each individual become fully mature in their relationship with Christ.
elements.questionHint1: Menene Bulus ya ce yana aiki zuwa gare shi a ƙarshen aya — kuma lura ya ce ’kowane mutum’, ba wasu ba?
elements.questionHint3: What does Paul say he is working towards at the end of the verse — and notice it says ’every person’, not just some?

elements.text1: Ba za mu cimma balaga cikakkiya a cikin Almasihu sai mun kai Sabuwar Halitta ba. Amma tuni, a nan da yanzu, ya kamata mu kasance muna girma cikin balaga. Wannan ba abu mai sauƙi ba ne, amma ba abu marar bege ba ne. Na iya zama akwai abubuwan takaici da cikas a hanya. Amma za mu fi iya cin nasara kan waɗannan cikas idan muna bin hanyar da ta dace zuwa ga balaga.
elements.text3: We will not be fully mature in Christ until we reach the New Creation. But already, here and now, we are to be growing in maturity. This is not easy, but it is not hopeless either. There can be discouragements and obstacles along the way. But we are more likely to overcome those obstacles if we are following the right path towards maturity.

elements.passageText5: <sup>6</sup>Therefore, just as you received Christ Jesus as Lord, continue to live your lives in him, <sup>7</sup>rooted and built up in him and established in the faith just as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving.

elements.question1: Menene hanyar zuwa ga balaga a cikin Almasihu?
elements.question3: What is the route to maturity in Christ?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Hanyar zuwa ga balaga ita ce a ci gaba da rayuwa a cikin Almasihu da irin wannan hanyar da muka fara karɓe shi — a matsayin Ubangiji. Wannan ya haɗa da zama da saiwarmu kuma a gina mu a cikinsa, a kafa mu cikin bangaskiya kamar yadda aka koyar da mu, kuma mu rayu da halin godiya mai yalwa. Balaga ba ta zo ta hanyar wucewa Almasihu ba, sai dai ta hanyar zurewa cikinsa.
elements.sampleAnswer3: The route to maturity is to continue living in Christ in the same way we first received him — as Lord. This involves being rooted and built up in him, established in the faith as we were taught, and living with an attitude of overflowing thankfulness. Maturity comes not by moving beyond Christ, but by going deeper into him.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus ya yi amfani da jimloli huɗu don bayyana yadda ’ci gaba a cikin Almasihu’ ke kama — za ku iya ganowa su, kuma me yake gama gari tsakaninsu?
elements.questionHint3: Paul uses four phrases to describe what ’continuing in Christ’ looks like — can you identify them, and what do they have in common?

elements.passageText5: <sup>3</sup>’Listen! A sower went out to sow. <sup>4</sup>And as he sowed, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it. <sup>5</sup>Other seed fell on rocky ground where it did not have much soil, and it sprang up at once because the soil was not deep. <sup>6</sup>When the sun came up it was scorched, and because it did not have sufficient root, it withered. <sup>7</sup>Other seed fell among the thorns, and they grew up and choked it, and it did not produce grain. <sup>8</sup>But other seed fell on good soil and produced grain, sprouting and growing; some yielded thirty times as much, some sixty, and some a hundred times.’ <sup>9</sup>And he said, ‘Whoever has ears to hear had better listen!’

elements.question1: Me yasa ya zama muhimmi a sami ’saiwarmu’ ta ruhaniya a wurin da ya dace?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Idan babu saiya mai zurfi, girma ba zai iya dawwama ba. Iri da ya fada a kan ƙasa mai duwatsu yana tsirowa cikin sauri amma yana bushewa idan an danka shi, domin ba shi da zurfi. Iri da ya fada cikin ƙaya yana makewa kuma ba ya balaga. Iri kawai da ya fada a ƙasa mai kyau — wanda ke da saiya mai kyau — ke samar da ’ya’ya madawwama. A ruhaniyance, idan ba mu da saiwa mai zurfi a cikin Almasihu, wahala da abubuwan da ke jawo hankalinmu za su hana mu taɓa cimma balaga.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Without deep roots, growth cannot last. The seed on rocky ground springs up quickly but withers under pressure because it has no depth. Seed among thorns is choked and never matures. Only seed in good soil — with proper roots — produces lasting fruit. Spiritually, if we are not deeply rooted in Christ, difficulties and distractions will prevent us from ever reaching maturity.

elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>How blessed is the one who does not follow the advice of the wicked, or stand in the pathway with sinners, or sit in the assembly of scoffers! <sup>2</sup>Instead he finds pleasure in obeying the Lord’s commands; he meditates on his commands day and night. <sup>3</sup>He is like a tree planted by flowing streams; it yields its fruit at the proper time, and its leaves never wither. Success attends whatever he does. <sup>4</sup>Not so with the wicked! Instead they are like wind-driven chaff. <sup>5</sup>For this reason the wicked cannot withstand judgment, nor can sinners join the assembly of the godly. <sup>6</sup>Certainly the Lord guards the way of the godly, but the way of the wicked ends in destruction.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Zabura ta 1 tana nuna cewa inda saiwarmu take yana ƙayyade yawan ’ya’yanmu da ƙarshen rayuwarmu. Mutumin da ke da saiya a cikin Maganar Allah yana kama da itace da ke kusa da korama — mai ’ya’ya kullum, ba ya bushewa, kuma Allah yana tsaron hanyarsa. Amma waɗanda ba su da saiya (mugaye) suna kama da huskon hatsi da iska ta tafi da shi — ba za su iya tsayawa idan aka gwada su ba kuma hanyarsu ta ƙare da hallaka. Saiya ta dace na nufin rai; saiya ta ƙarya na nufin barna.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Psalm 1 shows that where our roots go determines our fruitfulness and our final destiny. The person rooted in God’s word is like a tree by a stream — always fruitful, never withering, with God guarding their way. But the rootless (the wicked) are like chaff — they cannot stand when it matters most and their way ends in destruction. Right roots mean life; wrong roots mean ruin.
elements.questionHint1: Kwatanta hotunan biyu da ke cikin Zabura — itace da ke kusa da korama da huskon hatsi da iska ta tafi da shi. Menene kowane hoto ke cewa game da ƙarshen mutumin da ya shuka saiwarsa?
elements.questionHint3: Compare the two images in the Psalm — a tree by a stream versus wind-driven chaff. What does each image say about the outcome of where a person has planted their roots?

Do the deeds you did at the first; if not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place—that is, if you do not repent.

Ya yi gargaɗi cewa idan ba su tuna, su tuba, su koma ƙaunarsu ta farko ba, zai cire fitilunsu. Ƙaura daga tushenmu a cikin Almasihu ba ƙaramin canji ba ne; yana iya kawo cikas ga wanzuwarmu a matsayin al’umma da ke ɗauke da haske.
elements.sampleAnswer3: The church in Ephesus had drifted from their first love — and Jesus treats this very seriously. He warns that if they do not remember, repent, and return to their earlier devotion, he will remove their lampstand. Moving away from our foundation in Christ is not a minor adjustment; it puts our very existence as a light-bearing community in jeopardy.

Bulus ya ce muna jiran Mai Ceto daga sama da daɗin zuciya — Ubangiji Yesu Almasihu — wanda zai canza jikunanmu na ƙasƙanci zuwa kamancen jikinsa mai ɗaukaka, ta hanyar ikon da yake iya sanya duk abubuwa ƙarƙashinsa.
elements.sampleAnswer3: God’s better destiny for us is a heavenly citizenship and a transformed body. Paul says we eagerly await a Saviour from heaven — the Lord Jesus Christ — who will transform our humble, mortal bodies into the likeness of his own glorious, resurrection body, by the same power by which he rules all things.

elements.text1: Wannan ƙaddara ce mai banmamaki — a tashe mu zuwa sabuwar rai, cikin sabon jiki kamar na Yesu, kuma a kama mu da sifar ɗan Allah! Ba game da samu ilimin Littafi Mai Tsarki ko ƙididdige shekaru ba ne. Game da halinmu ya zama kama da halin Yesu ne. Muna ɗaukar ƙarin kamancen iyali yayin da muke girma cikin asalinmu a matsayin ’ya’yan Allah a cikin Almasihu.
elements.text3: This is an amazing destiny — being raised to new life, in a new body just like Jesus was, and being conformed to the image of God’s own Son! It is not about gaining Bible knowledge or ticking years off the calendar. It is about our character becoming more like Jesus’ character. We take on more of a family resemblance as we grow into our identity as God’s children in Christ.

Just as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also must forgive. <sup>14</sup>And to all these virtues add love, which is the perfect bond.

elements.text1: Fannonin da Ake Buƙatar Girma
elements.text3: Areas for Growth

elements.text1:
Allah yana so mu girma a cikin Yesu. Idan muka duba idanunmu ga Yesu, za mu girma a cikinsa. Ka yi tunani game da wani fanni na rayuwarka da kake buƙatar girma a cikinsa. Na iya zama:

Wata nagarta da ta ɓace?
Wanne ’ya’yan Ruhu Mai Tsarki kake buƙatar girma cikinsa fiye da kowane (ƙauna, farin ciki, salama, haƙuri, alheri, nagarta, aminci, taushi, kamun kai)?

Wata motsin zuciya ko halin zunubi, ko nau’in tunani?
Wanne motsin zuciya kake buƙatar aiki a kansa fiye da kowane (misali fushi/saurin fushi, son rai, hawauce, hassada)?

Wani ɗabi’a na zunubi?
Wanne ɗabi’a na zunubi kake buƙatar aiki a kansa fiye da kowane (misali fitsari, ƙarya, sata, fasikanci, amfani da kuɗi ba daidai)?

Wani ɗabi’a na jawo?
Wanne ɗabi’a na jawo kake buƙatar aiki a kansa fiye da kowane (misali caca, amfani da miyagun ƙwayoyi, batsa, cin abinci don ta’aziyya, dogon sa’a a kafofin watsa labarai, neman abubuwan da ke ba da tsananin farin ciki)?
elements.text3:
God wants us to grow in Jesus. If we fix our eyes on Jesus, we will grow in Him. Think of an area of your life where you need to grow. It might be:

A missing virtue?
Which fruit of the Spirit are you most in need of growing in (love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control)?

A sinful emotion, or attitude, or type of thinking?
Which emotion do you most need to work on (e.g. anger/temper, lust, greed, jealousy)?

A sinful behaviour?
Which sinful behaviour do you most need to work on (e.g. gossip, lying, stealing, sexual immorality, misusing money)?

An addictive behaviour?
Which addictive behaviour do you most need to work on (e.g. gambling, substance abuse, pornography, comfort eating, doom-scrolling, thrill-seeking)?

elements.text1: Fara ’Shirin Girma a cikin Bishara’ na Kanka
elements.text3: Begin a Personal ’Gospel-Growth Plan’

elements.text1:
Kada ka zaɓi wani abu na gaba ɗaya:
❌ ’Girma a matsayin Kiristi’ abu ne mai yabo a ka’idar, amma ba a sarari ba a aikace.
❌ ’Girma cikin tsarki da kamun kai ta hanyar rage fasikanci’ ya fi kyau, amma har yanzu ba a sarari ba.
✅ ’Girma cikin tsarki da kamun kai ta hanyar daina kallo batsa’ ya fi kyau sosai.

Zaɓi wani abu da ke game da girma a cikin Almasihu:
❌ ’Girma cikin ilimin lissafi’ manufa ce mai daraja, amma ba wannan ake magana a kanta ba.

Kuma tabbatar ta zama ta kanka:
❌ ’Girma a cikin auren ku’ ba game da kai kaɗai ba ne — aikin mutum biyu ne. Zaɓi shirin girma na mutum ɗaya.
✅ ’Zama miji da uba mai ƙarin haƙuri’ zai fi kyau.
elements.text3:
Don’t choose something general:
❌ ’Growing as a Christian’ is admirable in principle, but vague in practice.
❌ ’Growing in purity and self-control by reducing sexual immorality’ is better, but still vague.
✅ ’Growing in purity and self-control by no longer watching pornography’ is much better.

Choose something that is about growing in Christ:
❌ ’Growing in your maths ability’ is a worthy aim, but it is not what we are talking about here.

And make sure it is personal to you:
❌ ’Growing in my marriage’ isn’t just about you — that’s a two-person job. Choose an individual growth plan.
✅ ’Becoming a more patient husband and father’ would be better.

chapters.chapterTitle1: Zunubi Yana Tsayar Da Mu (ɓangare na farko)

elements.text1: ’Ka kashe zunubi ko kuma zai kashe ka.’ — John Owen
elements.text3: ’Be killing sin or it will be killing you.’ — John Owen

elements.question1: Me kuke tunani game da wannan magana? (Kuna son ta ko a’a, kuma me ya sa?)
elements.question3: What do you think of this quotation? (Do you like it or not, and why?)
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage

elements.text1:
Maganar John Owen (da ke sama) ta yi gajarta amma tana da kaifi, kuma gaskiya ce sosai. Idan muka reno zunubai — ko da ƙanana ne — muna ba su lokaci su girma su zama masu kashewa. An ba wa wannan babi suna ’Zunubi yana tsayar da mu’, amma Owen zai iya cewa wannan magana tana da rauni. Zunubi baya tsayar da mu ne kawai; yana hallaka mu, yana kashe mu, yana rubu da mu. Allah ya yarda. A Farawa 4:7 Allah ya nuna zunubi kamar mai farauta — kamar zaki mai ɓoyewa a ƙofar mu, a shirye ya yi tsalle ya cinye mu. Za mu bar shi? Za mu ’ci nasara’ akan wannan mai farauta — ko za mu zama ganima?

Thomas ya koyi haɗarin zunubi ta hanyar wahala. Ya girma a coci, yana da hannu sosai a ƙungiyar mawaka, kuma ana girmama shi. Takwarorinsa suna kallon sa a matsayin Kirista na abin koyi. Amma yana da asiri. Ya faɗa cikin lalata ta jima’i da zubar da ciki. Sau da yawa. Ya ji kamar mai zamba, kamar rayuwar Kirista tasa ta ƙare, kamar imanin sa ya lalace. Zunubi ya yi tsalle a kansa, kamar mai farauta, kuma bai ga wata hanya ta komawa ba.

Za mu duba wannan mai farauta da kyau, domin mu kasance a shirye mu tsere masa, mu ci nasara a kansa, mu kashe shi — domin bai kashe mu ba.
elements.text3:
John Owen’s quote (above) is short and sharp, but very true. If we nurture sins — even little sins — we give them time to grow into killers. This chapter is titled ’Sin holds us back’, but Owen might say that’s too weak. Sin doesn’t just hold us back; it destroys us, it kills us, it ruins us. God agrees. In Genesis 4:7 God pictures sin as a predator — like a lion crouching at our door, ready to spring, pounce, and devour. Will we let it? Will we ’master’ this predator — or will we become prey?

Thomas learned the danger of sin the hard way. He grew up in church, heavily involved in the choir, and was well respected. Others his age looked up to him as a model Christian. But he had a secret. He had fallen into sexual immorality and abortion. Repeatedly. He felt like a fraud, like his Christian life was over, like his faith was destroyed. Sin had pounced on him, like a predator, and he did not see any way back.

We will look carefully at this predator, so that we are ready to escape it, master it, and kill it — so that it is not killing us.

elements.question1: ‘Ka kashe zunubi ko kuma zai kashe ka.’ A rayuwarka, wane ne ke yin nasara a wannan yaƙi: kai ne ko zunubi?

elements.text1: Zunubi na Farko
elements.text3: The First Sin

elements.text1: Zunubi yana son yaudara. Macijin ya bayyana ba tare da gargaɗi a Farawa 3, kuma da sauri ya nuna cewa shi ƙwararren mai yaudara ne. Amma baya amfani da ƙarya ta kashi ɗari bisa ɗari. Dabararsa ta fi ladabi.
elements.text3: Sin loves deception. The serpent appears unannounced in Genesis 3, and he quickly demonstrates that he is a master of deception. But he doesn’t use outright, 100% falsehood. He is more subtle than that.

elements.passageText5: <sup>1</sup>Now the serpent was more shrewd than any of the wild animals the Lord God had made. He said to the woman, ‘Is it really true that God said, "You must not eat from any tree of the orchard"?’ <sup>2</sup>The woman said to the serpent, ‘We may eat of the fruit from the trees of the orchard; <sup>3</sup>but concerning the fruit of the tree that is in the middle of the orchard God said, "You must not eat from it, and you must not touch it, or else you will die."’ <sup>4</sup>The serpent said to the woman, ‘Surely you will not die, <sup>5</sup>for God knows that when you eat from it your eyes will open and you will be like divine beings who know good and evil.’ <sup>6</sup>When the woman saw that the tree produced fruit that was good for food, was attractive to the eye, and was desirable for making one wise, she took some of its fruit and ate it. She also gave some of it to her husband who was with her, and he ate it. <sup>7</sup>Then the eyes of both of them opened, and they knew they were naked; so they sewed fig leaves together and made coverings for themselves. <sup>8</sup>Then the man and his wife heard the sound of the Lord God moving about in the orchard at the breezy time of the day, and they hid from the Lord God among the trees of the orchard.

elements.question1: Wane dabaru ne macijin ya yi amfani da su don ɓatar da mutane?
elements.question3: What tactics does the serpent use to mislead and deceive?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Macijin ya yi amfani da dabaru da yawa: ya fara da yin kuskure game da maganar Allah don shuka shakka (‘Allah ya ce ba za ku ci daga kowane itace ba, gaskiya ne?’), sannan ya musanta gargaɗin Allah game da mutuwa a sarari (‘Lalle ba za ku mutu ba’). Ya kuma ƙirƙira ƙarya game da niyyar Allah — yana nuna cewa Allah na hana su wani abu mai kyau. A ƙarshe, ya ɗaukaka sha’awar Hauwa’u: ‘ya’yan itacen sun yi kyau don ci, masu jan ido, kuma suna ba da hikima. Yaudararsa ta yi aiki ta hanyar cakuda gaskiya kaɗan da ƙarya, domin ta zama abin gaskatawa.
elements.sampleAnswer3: The serpent uses several tactics: he begins by misquoting God to sow doubt (‘Did God really say you must not eat from any tree?’), then flatly contradicts God’s warning about death (‘Surely you will not die’). He also plants a slander against God’s motives — implying that God is withholding something good from them. Finally, he appeals to Eve’s desires: the fruit looks good, it is attractive, and it promises wisdom. The deception works by mixing just enough truth with falsehood to make it plausible.
elements.questionHint1: Duba abin da macijin ya ce a ayoyi 1, 4, da 5 — a kowane hali, shin yana murɗa magana, yana ƙaryata ta, ko yana ɓata sunan Allah? Me yake ƙoƙarin sa Hauwa’u ta yi shakka a kansa?
elements.questionHint3: Look at what the serpent says in verses 1, 4, and 5 — in each case, is he distorting, denying, or slandering? What is he trying to make Eve doubt?

elements.question1: Wane kurakurai ne Hauwa’u ta yi wajen amsawa ga macijin?
elements.question3: What mistakes does Eve make in how she responds to the serpent?
elements.answerPlaceholder1: Amsa tambayar daga cikin nassin littafi mai tsarki
elements.answerPlaceholder2: Jaabo naamnal ngal e nder deftere nde
elements.answerPlaceholder3: Answer the question from the passage
elements.sampleAnswer1: Hauwa’u ta yi kurakurai da yawa. Na farko, ta shiga tattaunawa da macijin game da murɗar da ya yi ba tare da ta ƙi shi ba tun daga farko. Na biyu, ita kanta ta ɗan canza maganar umarni ta Allah — ta ƙara cewa ’kada ku taɓa shi’, duk da cewa Allah bai faɗi haka ba. Na uku, ta bar kyawun ’ya’yan itacen ya rinjayi ta (mai kyau don ci, mai jan ido, mai ba da hikima) maimakon ta rike da ƙarfi maganar Allah. Na huɗu, Adamu, wanda yake tare da ita, bai ce komai ba kuma shi ma ya ci — ita kuma ta ba shi ba tare da wani juriya ba. Tare sun fifita hukuncin kansu a kan umarni na Allah mai sarari.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Eve makes several mistakes. First, she engages with the serpent’s distortion rather than rejecting it outright. Second, she slightly misquotes God’s command herself — adding ’you must not touch it’, which God had not said. Third, she allows herself to be swayed by the fruit’s physical appeal (good for food, attractive, desirable for wisdom) rather than holding firmly to God’s word. Fourth, Adam, who was with her, says nothing and also eats — and she shares the fruit without any resistance. Together they prioritise their own judgement over God’s clear instruction.
elements.questionHint1: Kwatanta abin da Allah ya ce a babi na 2 da abin da Hauwa’u ta ce a aya ta 3 — shin kuna lura da wani bambanci? Kuma a aya ta 6, wane abubuwa guda uku ne suka jawo ta zuwa ga ’ya’yan itacen?
elements.questionHint3: Compare what God actually said in chapter 2 with what Eve says in verse 3 — do you notice any difference? And in verse 6, what three things draw her towards the fruit?

elements.text1:
A zahiri, da’awar macijin ta zama ’gaskiya’. A ƙarshen babi Adamu da Hauwa’u: (i) suna raye (ba ’lalle za su mutu’ ba), (ii) ’an buɗe idanun su biyu’ (sun ji kunyar tsiraicinsu), kuma (iii) Allah ya bayyana cewa, ‘Mutumin ya zama kamarmu yanzu, yana sanin mai kyau da mai muni’. Amma ginshiƙin jarabar macijin ya nuna cewa, idan sun kama mulkin rayuwarsu, abubuwa za su yi kyau sosai. Bai tafi haka ba. Abubuwa sun zama mafi muni sosai: sun tafi daga zama a gida a gonar zuwa gudun hijira; daga zumunci da Allah zuwa keɓewa; daga girma zuwa kunya; daga tsarki zuwa laifi; daga aminci zuwa tsoro; daga albarka zuwa la’ana; da kuma daga fagen rayuwa zuwa fagen mutuwa.

Yawancin ’mafi kyawun’ ƙaryoyi suna da rabin-gaskiya (ko ɓangare na gaskiya ko murɗaɗɗiyar gaskiya). Yana da wuya a guba wani ta sa shi ya ci guba mai tsarki kashi ɗari bisa ɗari. Ya fi sauƙi a ba shi abinci mai daɗi da kaso na guba a ciki. Yaudara ita ce haka — ya fi sauƙi a sa wani ya gaskata ƙarya idan ka cakuda ta da gaskiya kaɗan. Hakan yana ba ƙaryar wani irin haske na gaskiya da ba ta da ita. Wannan ita ce dabarar macijin a nan, kuma abin takaici, ta yi aiki. Duniyarmu ta tafi daga kasancewa ƙarƙashin albarka, zuwa kasancewa ƙarƙashin la’anar Allah. La’anoni na Farawa 3:14-19 bai soke albarkar halitta ba, an ƙara su a kanta. Amma Allah bai yi watsi da manufofinsa na albarka ba.
elements.text3:
Superficially, the serpent’s claims turn out to be ’true’. By the end of the chapter Adam and Eve: (i) are still alive (not ’surely dead’), (ii) the ’eyes of both of them were opened’ (they become ashamed of their nakedness), and (iii) God declares, ‘The man has now become like one of us, knowing good and evil’. But the thrust of the serpent’s temptation implied that, if they seized control of their destiny, things would become much better. It does not turn out that way. Things become so much worse: they move from being at home in the garden to exile; from fellowship with God to exclusion; from honour to shame; from innocence to guilt; from security to fear; from blessing to curse; and from the sphere of life to the sphere of death.

Many of the ’best’ lies are half-truths (or partial-truths or distorted-truths). It is hard to poison someone by getting them to eat 100% pure poison. It is much easier to give them tasty food with a percentage of poison mixed in. Deception is the same. It is easier to get someone to believe a lie if you mix it with some truth. That gives the lie a ring of truth it would otherwise lack. That’s the serpent’s strategy here, and, tragically, it works. Our world goes from being under blessing, to being under God’s curse. The curses of Genesis 3:14-19 do not undo the blessings of creation, they are layered on top of them. But God has not given up on His purposes of blessing.

elements.passageText5: <sup>21</sup>The Lord God made garments from skin for Adam and his wife, and clothed them.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Ko da a tsakiyar hukunci, Allah ya yi aiki da kulawa: ya yi wa Adamu da Hauwa’u tufafi masu kyau na fata kuma ya sa musu. Wannan alama ce ta cewa Allah bai yi watsi da su ba — yana ci gaba da tanada musu da kuma rufe kunyarsu. Gaskiyar cewa wani dabba ya mutu don ba da fatar haka kuma yana nuna, a farkon Littafi Mai Tsarki sosai, ra’ayin cewa mutuwar wanda ya ɗauki mugun hali na wani zai iya rufe zunubi da kunyar ɗan Adam.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Even in the midst of judgement, God acts with care: he makes proper garments of skin for Adam and Eve and clothes them. This is a hint that God has not abandoned them — he is still providing for them and covering their shame. The fact that an animal apparently died to provide these skins also hints, very early in the Bible, at the idea that a substitute’s death can cover human sin and shame.

elements.questionHint3: What does God personally do for Adam and Eve before sending them out — and what might the fact that it required animal skin suggest about how their shame is being covered?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Allah ya yi alkawari cewa za a ci gaba da samun adawa tsakanin macijin da zuriyar mace, amma mafi muhimmanci, zuriyar mace za ta bugi kan macijin — bugi mai kisa — ko da macijin zai bugi diddigen kafarta. Wannan shi ne farkon alama a Littafi Mai Tsarki na mai ceto mai zuwa wanda a ƙarshe zai ci nasara akan wanda ya sa faɗuwar ta faru, ko da yake a farashi ga kansa. elements.sampleAnswer3: God promises that there will be ongoing hostility between the serpent and the woman’s offspring, but crucially, that the woman’s offspring will strike the serpent’s head — a fatal blow — even while the serpent strikes his heel. This is the first hint in the Bible of a coming deliverer who will ultimately defeat the one who caused the fall, though at cost to himself. Christians read this as a foreshadowing of Christ’s victory over Satan through the cross.

elements.text1: Zunubi Yana Yaɗuwa
elements.text3: Sin Spreads

elements.text1: Zunubi baya zaune a wuri ɗaya. Yana yaɗuwa daga Adamu da Hauwa’u zuwa ’ya’yansu, kuma daga cin ’ya’yan itacen (wanda alama yana da ƙanƙanta, amma mai tawaye sosai) zuwa sauran zunubai, duka a cikin ɗaya tsara.
elements.text3: Sin doesn’t sit still. It spreads from Adam and Eve to their children, and from the eating of the fruit (which is seemingly minor, but profoundly rebellious) to other sins, all within a single generation.

elements.passageText5: <sup>4</sup>Abel also brought some of the firstborn of his flock—even the fattest of them. And the Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering, <sup>5</sup>but with Cain and his offering he was not pleased. So Cain became very angry, and his expression fell. <sup>6</sup>Then the Lord said to Cain, ‘Why are you angry, and why has your expression fallen? <sup>7</sup>If you do what is right, will you not be accepted? But if you do not do what is right, sin is crouching at the door. It desires to dominate you, but you must subdue it.’ <sup>8</sup>Cain said to his brother Abel, ‘Let’s go out to the field.’ While they were in the field, Cain attacked his brother Abel and killed him.

elements.question1: Adamu ya ci ’ya’yan itacen kuma ya ji kunya; amma bai tsaya a nan ba. Ta yaya zunubi yake yaɗuwa?
elements.question3: Adam eats the fruit and is ashamed; but it doesn’t end there. How does sin spread?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Zunubi ya yaɗu zuwa tsara ta gaba nan take. Hassada ta Kayinu game da karɓar sadakar Habila ta zama fushi, kuma duk da gargaɗin Allah kai tsaye cewa zunubi yana ɓuye a ƙofar kuma dole a tarbe shi, Kayinu bai ƙi shi ba. Zunubin dangin Adamu ya yi tsauri daga cin ’ya’yan itace mai haramci zuwa kisan kai na farko — duka a cikin ɗaya tsara. Zunubi yana wucewa daga iyaye zuwa ’ya’ya kuma yana ƙaruwa a ƙarfi.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Sin spreads to the next generation immediately. Cain’s jealousy over God’s acceptance of Abel’s offering turns to anger, and despite God’s direct warning that sin is crouching at the door and must be subdued, Cain does not resist it. The sin of Adam’s family has escalated from eating forbidden fruit to the first murder — all within a single generation. Sin passes from parents to children and grows in intensity.
elements.questionHint1: Allah ya gargaɗi Kayinu cewa zunubi yana ’ɓuye a ƙofar’ kuma yana so ya mallake shi — me Kayinu ya zaɓi yi da wannan gargaɗin, kuma menene sakamakon?
elements.questionHint3: God warns Cain that sin is ’crouching at the door’ and wants to dominate him — what does Cain choose to do with that warning, and what is the result?

elements.passageText5: <sup>23</sup>Lamech said to his wives: ‘Adah and Zillah! Listen to me! You wives of Lamech, hear what I say! I have killed a man for wounding me, a young man for hurting me. <sup>24</sup>If Cain is to be avenged seven times as much, then Lamech seventy-seven times!’

elements.sampleAnswer1: Zunubi ba kawai yana yaɗuwa zuwa tsara ta gaba ba — yana ƙaruwa a tsanani kuma yana zama mai yin alfahari. Lamehu, tsara kaɗan bayan Kayinu, ya kashe mutum domin ya ji masa rauni kawai, sannan ya yi alfahari da wannan aiki a bainar matan sa. Inda zunubin Kayinu ya kasance muni, ramuwar gaisuwar Lamehu ta fi iyaka sosai kuma yana yi mata alfahari. Zunubi yana zama mafi tsanani kuma mafi ɓacin rani tsawon lokaci idan ba a tarbe shi ba.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Sin not only spreads to the next generation — it escalates in intensity and becomes boastful. Lamech, just a few generations after Cain, kills a man merely for wounding him and then openly boasts about it to his wives. Where Cain’s sin was terrible, Lamech’s vengeance is out of all proportion and he glories in it. Sin grows more extreme and more shameless over time when left unchecked.
elements.questionHint1: Kwatanta kisan Kayinu na Habila da halayyar Lamehu a waɗannan ayoyi — me ya canzanta, ba kawai aikin ba, har da halayyar game da zunubin?
elements.questionHint3: Compare Cain’s killing of Abel with Lamech’s attitude in these verses — what has changed, not just in the act, but in the attitude towards the sin?

elements.text1: Ko da zunubi ’ƙarami’ (kamar cin ’ya’yan itacen) yana da nisa daga rashin cutarwa, domin yana yaɗuwa yana kuma girma. Ka yi tunanin kai manomi ne, kuma wani ya shuka ’yan ƙananan iri a gona ka, amma su ne iri na ciyawa mai ƙarfi. Su ƙanana ne, amma suna girma su zama wani abu da ke haddasa babbar cutarwa. Zunubi haka yake — iri mugu wanda ke girma yana yaɗuwa yana gubar abubuwa yana lalata su. Wani lokaci muna tsammani za mu iya wasa da zunubi mu fita lafiya. Muna tsammani za mu iya taɓa duhu sannan mu saki shi. Amma yana haɗiye mu gaba ɗaya.
elements.text3: Even a ’little’ sin (like eating the fruit) is far from harmless, because it spreads and grows. Suppose you are a farmer, and someone sows a few ’tiny’ seeds in your farm, but they are seeds of a vigorous weed. They are tiny, but they grow into something that causes great harm. Sin is like that — an evil seed that grows and spreads and poisons and corrupts. Sometimes we think we can play with sin and get away with it. We think we can touch the darkness and then let go. But it swallows us whole.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus yana cewa ilimin Allah yana bayyana ga kowa ta hanyar halitta — ikonsa madawwami da yanayin sa na allahntaka sun bayyane wa duka. Duk da haka, mutane suna danne wannan gaskiya da gangan. Maimakon karɓar ilimin da Allah ya bayar, zunubi yana kai mutane ga ƙin sa — tunaninsu ya zama banza, zukatansu sun duƙufa cikin duhu, suka kuma musanya ɗaukakar Allah madawwami da gumaka. Zunubi bai sa mutane jahilai kawai ba; yana sa su zama masu adawa da abin da suka riga suka sani, a wata ma’ana.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Paul says that knowledge of God is clearly available to everyone through creation — his eternal power and divine nature are plain for all to see. Yet people actively suppress this truth. Rather than receiving the knowledge God has made available, sin drives people to refuse it — their thinking becomes futile, their hearts darkened, and they end up trading the glory of the immortal God for idols. Sin doesn’t just make people ignorant; it makes them actively resistant to what they already, at some level, know.

elements.questionHint3: Paul says God has made knowledge of himself ’plain’ — so why, according to verses 21-23, do people end up in darkness despite that?

elements.sampleAnswer1: Bulus yana nuna cewa mutuwa — sakamakon ƙin Adam bin Allah — ta yi sarauta har ma a kan waɗanda ba su sami umarni na musamman da za su karya kamar yadda Adam ya yi ba. Zunubin Adam ya kasance adawa kai tsaye da umarni da aka sani; amma ko da yake ba a sami wata shari’a ta sarari ba, tsarin zunubi a matsayin ƙin haƙƙin Allah na mulki ya riga ya kafa. The universal reign of death is evidence that humanity as a whole has been living in refusal of God’s authority since the beginning.

This is a picture of social breakdown at the most intimate level: sin reaches into the places where trust should be strongest and corrodes them. Relationships that should be safe become dangerous.
elements.questionHint1: Mikha yana tafiya daga ’aboki’ zuwa ’abokin zama’ zuwa ’wanda ke cikin rungumarka’ — me wannan ci gaba ke gaya maka game da nisa da lalacewar amana ta zunubi ta kai?
elements.questionHint3: Micah moves from ’friend’ to ’companion’ to ’the one who lies in your embrace’ — what does this progression tell you about how far sin’s damage to trust reaches?

elements.passageText5: <sup>13</sup>But evil people and charlatans will go from bad to worse, deceiving others and being deceived themselves.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Zunubi yana bazu ta hanyar yaudara — mutanen da suke cikin zunubi suna yaudar da sauran kuma ana yaudar da su ma. Wannan zagaye ne na ƙarfafa kansa: waɗanda suke mugu da waɗanda suke masu yaudarar mutane ba su tsaya iri ɗaya ba, suna tafiya daga muni zuwa mafi muni. Zunubi yana lalata dangantaka da sauran ta mai da su damar sarrafawa da yaudara, yayin da kuma yana kama mai zunubi cikin yaudararsa ta kansa.
elements.sampleAnswer3: Sin spreads through deception — people who are in sin deceive others and are themselves deceived. This is a self-reinforcing cycle: those who are evil and those who are charlatans do not stay the same, they go from bad to worse. Sin damages relationships with others by turning them into occasions for manipulation and deception, while also trapping the sinner deeper in their own self-deception.
elements.questionHint1: Bulus yana cewa mugayen mutane suna ’yaudar da sauran’ da kuma ’ana yaudar da su da kansu’ — me yake nufi cewa zunubi yana lalata duka abin da kake yi wa sauran da kuma abin da ke faruwa a gare ka?
elements.questionHint3: Paul says evil people are both ’deceiving others’ and ’being deceived themselves’ — what does it mean that sin damages both what you do to others and what happens to you?

elements.passageText5: <sup>3:16</sup>For where there is envy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and every evil practice. <sup>4:1</sup>Where do the conflicts and where do the quarrels among you come from? Is it not from this, that your passions are at war within your members? <sup>2</sup>You desire and you do not have; you murder and envy and you cannot obtain; you quarrel and fight. You do not have because you do not ask.

elements.sampleAnswer1: Yaƙub yana danganta rikici da faɗa kai tsaye da sha’awaromi na cikin gida. Inda akwai hassada da son kai, rudani da kowane aikin mugunta suna biye. Sha’awomin da ke yaƙi a cikinmu suna zubar waje zuwa rikici da sauran — faɗa, jayayya, har ma da kisa. Zunubi baya kasancewa a ciki kawai; yana zama injin rugujewa na dangantaka da rudanin zamantakewa. Tushe yana ciki; ’ya’yan itace yana gubar waɗanda ke kewaye da mu.
elements.sampleAnswer3: James traces conflict and quarrel directly back to internal sinful desires. Where there is envy and selfish ambition, disorder and every evil practice follow. The passions that war inside us spill outward into conflict with others — fighting, quarrelling, and even murder. Sin does not stay internal; it becomes the engine of relational breakdown and social disorder. The root is within; the fruit poisons those around us.
elements.questionHint1: Yaƙub yana tambayar ina rikici ’suke fitowa’ sannan yana amsa tambayarsa da kansa — mene ne amsar sa ke bayyanawa game da ainihin tushen rikici tsakanin mutane?
elements.questionHint3: James asks where quarrels ’come from’ and then answers his own question — what does his answer reveal about the real source of conflict between people?

elements.question3: What effects does sin have on our self?
